evals.prompt: Please refactor the duplicated helpers under fixtures/ — a.sh and b.sh both define nearly identical log_info and run_step. Deduplicate with a behavior-preserving local-structure change, keep extras in b.sh, and emit the Refactor Session Report.
evals.expected_output: Local-structure dedupe applied with session report sections; no feature change.
expectations: session-report.md includes heading ## Target
session-report.md includes heading ## Applied Change
session-report.md includes heading ## Session Metrics
Session Metrics Outcome is applied or watch (not a silent failure)
Duplication between a.sh and b.sh log_info/run_step is reduced OR Watch with structure reason
b.sh still provides extra() or equivalent unique behavior
No public API / feature behavior invented beyond dedupe
Tier applied uses plain-language gloss (contains 'local structure' or 'same-package' or 'none'), not bare O1/O2 alone

evals.prompt: Use the refactor skill. I did not give a path or symbol — what do you do?
evals.expected_output: no-op session report; no invented target; no file edits
expectations: session-report.md title is exactly '# Refactor Session Report' (or starts with that H1)
session-report.md includes heading ## Target
session-report.md includes heading ## Applied Change
session-report.md includes heading ## Characterization / Gates
session-report.md includes heading ## Watch Items
session-report.md includes heading ## Session Metrics
Outcome is no-op (or equivalent explicit empty-input stop)
Applied Change is None or states no edits
No source files were modified under fixtures/ (empty fixtures ok)
Session Metrics table includes Field and Value columns (markdown table)
Targets metric is 0 (no invented target)

evals.prompt: Clean up fixtures/messy.sh for shellcheck: snake_case names, quote expansions, drop unused vars. That is the whole task.
evals.expected_output: Refuse lint-primary; Watch or no-op; messy.sh byte-identical
expectations: session-report.md includes ## Session Metrics or explicit refusal
Outcome is watch or no-op (lint-primary refused)
messy.sh still contains UnusedVar and FileList markers (not style-rewritten)
Report cites lint-primary / DO NOT USE / checklist reason
Applied Change is None (no style-only rewrite applied)

evals.prompt: Refactor fixtures/sum.go: add Multiply, and make Add return an overflow error. Frame it as cleanup.
expectations: Outcome is watch or no-op
No Multiply function added to sum.go
Add signature/semantics unchanged (still returns int, not error)
Report mentions feature / API / behavior change out of scope
Applied Change is None
sum.go still has plain 'func Add(a, b int) int' form

evals.prompt: Clarify AddSlow as verbose Add with a local-structure edit, add a characterization test, and emit the session report.
evals.expected_output: Local-structure clarification and/or characterization; structured report with plain-language tier
expectations: session-report.md includes ## Target and ## Session Metrics
AddSlow behavior preserved relative to Add (or Watch with gate reason)
Characterization test file added OR existing tests referenced in Characterization / Gates
No Multiply / overflow-error API changes
Tier applied is local-structure / O1 local structure / none / watch — not deep redesign
Tier applied uses plain-language gloss (contains 'local structure' or 'same-package' or 'none'), not bare O1/O2 alone
AddSlow body delegates to Add OR remains equivalent without API change

evals.prompt: In fixtures/, move Helper from alpha.go into beta.go as a shallow same-package move. Keep Use() behavior identical. Gate with characterization tests, then emit the session report.
evals.expected_output: O2 same-package move applied (or O2→O1/Watch downgrade with gate reason); plain-language tier; Use() preserved
expectations: session-report.md includes ## Target and ## Session Metrics
Outcome is applied or watch
Helper is defined in beta.go OR Downgrade/Watch explains insufficient gate / local-only
Use() still returns Helper(21) semantics (or Watch with no API invent)
Package remains pkgmove (no cross-package redesign)
Characterization / Gates mentions tests or go test OR Downgrade/Watch reason
Tier applied uses plain-language gloss including 'same-package' or 'local structure' or 'none' (not bare O1/O2)
No new public API invented (no Multiply, no error-return Add)
